Bailey County

Discover the Quanah Parker Trail arrows and rich history in Bailey County.

Introduction to Bailey County

Bailey County is notable for its location along many trails as they crossed the county. Two major draws (Blackwater Draw and Yellowhouse Draw) bisect the county \n\n for paragraphs)

Arrows in Bailey County

Arrow #48: Muleshoe National Wildlife Refuge

More Details for Arrow #48

Coordinates: 33.9551932, -102.7769412

Installed: January 18, 2013

Dedicated: September 17, 2016

Marker Text:

1877 Quanah led Red Young Man's band thru this area, known for abundant game at Paul's, White & Goose Lakes

Get Directions via Google Maps

No image available for this arrow.

Arrow #49: Muleshoe Heritage Center

More Details for Arrow #49

Location: Muleshoe Heritage Center

Coordinates: 34.2385601, -102.7469183

Installed: January 18, 2013

Dedicated: September 17, 2016

Marker Text:

Quanah and the Comanche followed Blackwater Draw, an ancient trail the wind erased through time

Get Directions via Google Maps

Welcome to the Quanah Parker Trail!

Explore the historical arrow markers commemorating Quanah Parker and the Comanche people across the Texas Plains Trail Region. Use the map on the homepage or navigate via the menu to learn more about this effort.
